methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Red 6, a Series B+ defense and security company that has raised over $100M, operates in a sector with 21 identified peers—2 public and 8 private of meaningful scale. The peer universe includes Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI among private companies, alongside public comparables DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS. This composition reflects the fragmented nature of frontier defense-tech, where stage-specific clustering remains sparse.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable here. Despite 21 same-sector peers identified, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for meaningful triangulation. Using an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity in defense-tech business models, revenue structures, and capital intensity across stages.

The analysis therefore cannot support a precise valuation range at this time. The fragmented peer set, combined with the absence of sufficient stage-matched comparables, creates material uncertainty around appropriate multiples or absolute value. To establish defensible valuation support, additional data on peer-level revenue, growth rates, and capital efficiency metrics would be required. Investors should treat any valuation estimate as preliminary pending deeper diligence into Red 6's specific revenue traction, customer concentration, and technical defensibility relative to the broader defense-tech landscape.
